title: Apply: ecological interactions
description: Apply your understanding of ecological interactions in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Read the passage below. Then, answer the question.**

Orcas off the coast of Norway often carry out a behavior called carousel feeding. During carousel feeding, a group of orcas surrounds a school of fish and herds them into a tight ball. Then, the orcas use their tails to whip the fish, which they eat.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which term best describes the interaction between orcas and fish described in the passage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: Two orcas swimming in the ocean.
options.caption: *Two orcas swimming*

choices.content: predatory
mutualistic
competitive

assessment_items.question_content:
**Read the passage below. Then, answer the question.**

Remoras are fish that have suction cup-like front fins. Remoras use these fins to attach to other animals, including lemon sharks. A lemon shark provides remoras with protection and food. In return, remoras remove parasites from the shark’s skin.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which term best describes the interaction between remoras and lemon sharks described in the passage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A lemon shark swimming with remora fish attached.
options.caption: *A lemon shark swimming with remoras attached*

choices.content: predatory
mutualistic
competitive

assessment_items.question_content:
**Read the passage below. Then, answer the question.**

Sea anemones are predatory animals that use their stinging tentacles to get their prey. However, clownfish are not affected by the anemone’s sting. So, clownfish are able to live within the anemone’s tentacles. Here, the clownfish get protection and access to food. In return, the clownfish remove parasites from the anemone.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which term best describes the interaction between anemones and clownfish described in the passage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation:
"Amphiprion ocellaris (Clown_anemonefish)" by Nick Hobgood, [CC BY-SA 3.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/deed.en)

Image url: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Amphiprion_ocellaris_(Clown_anemonefish)_by_Nick_Hobgood.jpg

options.alt: Two clownfish swimming among anemone tentacles.
options.caption: *Two clownfish swimming among anemone tentacles*

choices.content: predatory
mutualistic
competitive

assessment_items.question_content:
Lichens, such as the one shown below, are made up of two organisms: an algae and a fungus.

[[☃ image 1]]

The algae and fungus that make up a lichen cannot survive without each other.

**Which statement best describes the relationship between the algae and fungus that make up a lichen?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

choices.content: a mutualistic relationship in which both the algae and fungus rely on each other for survival
a competitive relationship in which the fungus out-competes the algae for resources
a predatory relationship in which the fungus relies on the algae as its prey

assessment_items.question_content:
Termites, such as the ones shown below, have unicellular protozoans living in their gut. These protozoans help the termites digest wood.

[[☃ image 1]]

Termites cannot survive without the protozoans. Similarly, the protozoans are found living only in the guts of termites.

**Which statement best describes the relationship between the termites and protozoans described above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

choices.content: a mutualistic relationship in which both the termites and protozoans rely on each other for survival
a competitive relationship in which the termites out-compete the protozoans for resources
a predatory relationship in which the termites rely on the protozoans as their prey

assessment_items.question_content:
*Pseudomyrmex* ants, such as the one shown below, are only found living on acacia trees. These trees provide the ants with food and shelter. The ants protect the trees from herbivores, which would damage and kill the tree if the ants were not there. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which statement best describes the relationship between the acacia trees and the ants described above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: "[Ant-Pseudomyrmex species, on Bull Thorn Acacia](https://www.flickr.com/photos/52450054@N04/8505045055/)" by Judy Gallagher, [CC BY 2.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0/deed.en)

options.alt: An ant on an acacia tree leaf.

choices.content: a mutualistic relationship in which both the trees and ants rely on each other for survival
a competitive relationship in which the ants out-compete the trees for resources
a predatory relationship in which the ants rely on the trees as their prey

assessment_items.question_content:
Blue crabs and mussels interact in a coastal ecosystem. When the size of the blue crab population gets bigger, the size of the mussel population tends to get smaller.

**Which of these best describes the interaction between blue crabs and mussels in the coastal ecosystem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a predatory interaction in which the blue crabs are the predators and the mussels are the prey
a mutualistic interaction in which the blue crabs and mussels help each other survive
a competitive interaction in which the mussels out-compete the blue crabs for food

assessment_items.question_content:
Box jellyfish and shrimp interact in a marine ecosystem. When the size of the box jellyfish population gets bigger, the size of the shrimp population tends to get smaller.

**Which of these best describes the interaction between box jellyfish and shrimp in the coastal ecosystem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a predatory interaction in which the jellyfish are the predators and the shrimp are the prey
a mutualistic interaction in which the jellyfish and shrimp help each other survive
a competitive interaction in which the shrimp out-compete the jellyfish for food

assessment_items.question_content:
Trapdoor spiders and snakes interact in a forest ecosystem. When the size of the trapdoor spider population gets bigger, the size of the snake population tends to get smaller.

**Which of these best describes the interaction between trapdoor spiders and snakes in the forest ecosystem?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a predatory interaction in which the spiders are the predators and the snakes are the prey
a mutualistic interaction in which the spiders and snakes help each other survive
a competitive interaction in which the snakes out-compete the spiders for food

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these interactions describes a competitive relationship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: large aphids removing small aphids from the best feeding sites on plant leaves
a beetle eating an aphid on a plant stem
ants getting food from aphids on a plant and protecting the aphids in return

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these interactions describes a competitive relationship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a male deer fighting with another male deer for a mate
a coyote eating a deer in a forest
a bird eating ticks and fleas off a deer in a meadow

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these interactions describes a competitive relationship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a squirrel defending a tree that produces lots of acorns from other squirrels
a hawk eating a squirrel in a foothill ecosystem
a squirrel burying acorns, or oak tree seeds, in the ground
